ddls:
CREATE TABLE "procedures" (
  "procedure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each procedure */,
  "procedure_name" TEXT /* Name of the procedure (e.g., Colonoscopy, Breast Surgery) */,
  "procedure_description" TEXT /* Description of the procedure */,
  "procedure_duration" INTEGER /* Average duration of the procedure in minutes */,
  "procedure_complexity" INTEGER /* Complexity level of the procedure (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "procedure_risk_level" TEXT /* Risk level of the procedure (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "procedure_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the procedure (e.g., Daily, Weekly, Monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("procedure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sub_groups" (
  "sub_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sub-group */,
  "sub_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the sub-group (e.g., Diagnostic, Therapeutic) */,
  "sub_group_description" TEXT /* Description of the sub-group */,
  "parent_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent sub-group */,
  "sub_group_hierarchy" INTEGER /* Level of the sub-group in the hierarchy (e.g., 1, 2, 3) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sub_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "procedure_sub_groups" (
  "procedure_sub_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each procedure-sub-group relationship */,
  "procedure_id" INTEGER /* ID of the procedure */,
  "sub_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sub-group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("procedure_sub_group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_procedure_sub_groups_sub_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("sub_group_id") REFERENCES sub_groups ("sub_group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_procedure_sub_groups_procedure_id FOREIGN KEY ("procedure_id") REFERENCES procedures ("procedure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "procedure_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "procedure_id" INTEGER /* ID of the procedure */,
  "sub_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sub-group */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the data */,
  "number_of_cases" INTEGER /* Number of cases performed */,
  "median_charge" REAL /* Median charge for the procedure */,
  "statewide_median_charge" REAL /* Statewide median charge for the procedure */,
  "total_charges" REAL /* Total charges for all cases */,
  "min_charge" REAL /* Minimum charge for the procedure */,
  "max_charge" REAL /* Maximum charge for the procedure */,
  "average_duration" INTEGER /* Average duration of the procedure in minutes */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state where the data is collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_procedure_data_procedure_id FOREIGN KEY ("procedure_id") REFERENCES procedures ("procedure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_procedure_data_sub_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("sub_group_id") REFERENCES sub_groups ("sub_group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_procedure_data_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the provider */,
  "provider_description" TEXT /* Description of the provider */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the provider */,
  "provider_type" TEXT /* Type of provider (e.g., Hospital, Clinic) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the provider (e.g., City, State) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website URL */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude */,
  "number_of_beds" INTEGER /* Number of beds (for hospitals) */,
  "specialties" TEXT /* Specialties offered by the provider (e.g., Cardiology, Oncology) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "provider_procedures" (
  "provider_procedure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider-procedure relationship */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* ID of the provider */,
  "procedure_id" INTEGER /* ID of the procedure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_procedure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provider_procedures_procedure_id FOREIGN KEY ("procedure_id") REFERENCES procedures ("procedure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provider_procedures_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES providers ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, provider) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., Read, Write, Admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Specific permissions granted to the user (e.g., View Reports, Download Data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES procedure_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "state_code" TEXT /* Two-letter code of the state */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state */,
  "median_income" INTEGER /* Median income of the state */,
  "healthcare_spending" INTEGER /* Total healthcare spending in the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documentation" (
  "documentation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each documentation entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was collected */,
  "data_validation_status" TEXT /* Status of data validation (e.g., Valid, Pending, Rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("documentation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_documentation_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES procedure_data ("data_id")
)
